methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.3B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies is a Series G climate and energy company that has raised $1.3B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private frontier-tech peers in Climate & Energy alongside one public comparable (LNZA), though a narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier limits multiples-based triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology proves applicable and forms the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised of $1.3B as a value proxy and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.6B. This method directly reflects the company's demonstrated ability to attract institutional capital and assumes comparable risk-adjusted returns to similar-stage deployment. Peer-median analysis was not pursued—while nine same-sector peers exist (including Helion, Redwood Materials, KoBolt Metals, and Electric Hydrogen), insufficient overlap with TAE's specific stage and valuation tier (fewer than three comparable datapoints meeting narrow filters) would introduce noise rather than signal.

The triangulated range spans $1.4B (low) to $1.9B (high), with $1.6B as the median. This analysis rests on a single applicable method, concentrating estimation risk. Key uncertainties include the company's path to commercialization, capital intensity of its core technology, and comparative burn rate relative to peer cohorts—data not available in the current dataset. Additional disclosure of near-term milestones or revenue metrics would substantially improve precision.
